mode_selection_reason: Three issues assigned to tinker (ISS-1285, ISS-2134, ISS-2175); pipeline otherwise healthy. ISS-2175 is the freshest and points at concrete audit work (ISS-2105 / EXP-425, 86 missing EXP IDs). Mode 1/2 quick checks returned green except for one expected build-drift integrity finding (already routed to decider) and one expected FUSE-staleness pattern (git-owned files lag FUSE by one workspace-sync round-trip).

mode_1_pipeline_health.fuse_freshness_note: FUSE workspace lags git by one workspace-sync round-trip — analyst-baby/curmudgeon/sloppytoppy summaries showed as 74h+ stale on FUSE but are 2h fresh in git. The Mode 1 check based on FUSE mtime would have falsely flagged 4 agents as stalled; using git-side freshness (clone) is the correct signal.
mode_1_pipeline_health.integrity_latest_finding: build-drift: decider commit 9f6fe08 (today 05:37Z) updated PRED-073 in data/predictions.json but did not run build.js — docs/index.html still shows stale 'standard geomagnetic models already predict' tldr. Next decider run must rebuild. Tinker does not own builds; deferring.

mode_2_infrastructure.status: yellow — disk pressure persistent but project-induced footprint is fine
mode_2_infrastructure.fuse_stale_interpretation: All four are git-owned files; workspace-sync correctly does NOT push them from FUSE. Drift will resolve on next decider→git→FUSE round-trip via build.js publish or workspace-sync's git→FUSE direction.
mode_2_infrastructure.disk_action: no cleanup — project footprint < 500MB threshold and /tmp/edit-clone is current-session (created this run)

mode_3_cost_engineering.status: not selected — Mode 4 had concrete assigned work

findings.title: ISS-2175 / ISS-2105 / EXP-425 (86 missing EXP IDs): audit complete, dominant cause is bulk-reservation drift, not lost work. PROP-053 filed.

evidence.smoking_gun: EXP-323 created 2026-05-13T12:04Z, EXP-349 created 2026-05-12T05:12Z (reverse-time). next_id was bulk-advanced past 349 without the intermediate 324–348 slots being written back. Same pattern on the 350–383 range (next jump to 384 on 2026-05-14T20:07).

recommended_decider_actions: Close ISS-2105 + ISS-2175 referencing PROP-053. Severity should drop from MAJOR to MODERATE (or INFO) on the audit reclassification.
If PROP-053 approved: route the audit script + reference doc creation to analyst-baby as a single mechanical EXP.
Optionally route the 7 orphan-file backfills (EXP-300, 330, 381, 382, 389, 390, 404) to analyst-baby as a low-priority consolidation.

findings.title: ISS-1285 (raw-text/02-wins.txt staleness): still real — file content is unchanged from 2026-03-12 / V50.6 / '39 confirmed' even though dome now has 69 WINs (gap of 30 WINs). No automated refresh mechanism exists.

evidence.raw_text_header_line_2: TITLE: Confirmed Wins (39) — Dome Cosmological Model
evidence.dome_current_state: Dome WIN count today: 69 (per decider summary 2026-05-24T05-36); raw-text shows 39. Gap: 30 WINs.
evidence.build_scripts_refresh_search: No `raw-text` references in build-scripts/ or scripts/; poller.md does not reference raw-text either. Three agents (analyst Mode 0, decider, curmudgeon) READ raw-text but no agent or script REFRESHES it.
evidence.consumer_impact: Per ISS-1285 routing note: live-site citations are already current (2026-04-20+ access dates), so raw-text staleness does not affect prose accuracy. However, analyst Mode 0 onboarding for new WINs (WIN-040+ era) is reading stale baseline content — agent fallback (fetch live HTML via GitHub API) catches this in practice, but it's an undocumented brittleness.

findings.recommended_action: Option A (preferred): add a small refresh script invoked daily by poller — fetch the dome's predictions.html and re-extract the raw-text snapshots. Option B: rename current raw-text/ to raw-text-v50.6-2026-03-12/ matching the existing archive pattern, and create an empty current raw-text/ with a manifest noting agents must fetch live. Option A is lower-risk; Option B documents the pattern but invites future drift. Tinker has not written this PROP — it's small enough to be a 5-line addition to poller.md. Recommend decider route to analyst-baby for a single PROP-write EXP.

findings.title: ISS-2134 (WIN-070 re-push loop): FIXED by PROP-048 dedup guard landed 2026-05-19. Issue is still open in tracker because no closure event ran.

evidence.dedup_guard_present: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md Step 1f Step 3 lines 170–198: 'WIN-NNN already integrated AND reviewed (review file exists); no priority-queue win-new push needed (PROP-048 dedup guard). Normal tracker rotation will pick it up for BAU re-review.'
evidence.decider_md_warning: monitor/prompts/decider.md lines 756–762: explicit comment 'naked copy-paste of this template for a win-new push will recreate the WIN-070 re-push loop (ISS-2126→ISS-2134)' — guard documented at site of risk.

findings.recommended_action: Decider should CLOSE ISS-2134 referencing PROP-048 commit. No further tinker work needed.

findings.title: dome-social hasn't run since 2026-05-21T03:36Z (~73h) despite enabled=true with daily 04:30 schedule. Three expected daily runs missed (05-22, 05-23, 05-24).

evidence.nextRunAt_anomaly: Schedule says 'daily' but nextRunAt is 4 days after lastRunAt. Possible causes: (a) the task was disabled-and-re-enabled in a way that pushed nextRunAt forward, (b) scheduler bug that recomputed nextRunAt from now+24h after a missed run instead of from the last expected slot, (c) operator manually skipped during the 5-21 workspace-sync disaster pause.

findings.recommended_action: Operator should re-check dome-social's enabled state and force a one-off catch-up run. This is a soft finding — social is the lowest-stakes agent (machine-readable layer, dome-author signal scanning) — but missed runs accumulate gaps in the daily social ledger that may matter if the dome author starts responding more visibly.

findings.title: Two scheduled tasks remain DISABLED: dome-sloppytoppy-score and dome-sloppytoppy-rewrite. Sloppytoppy summaries in git are 2h fresh — that means SOMEONE ran them manually or via a different path.

evidence.git_freshness: Both latest-*-summary.txt files were committed today (workspace-sync round-trip 2026-05-24T03:07Z, commit 39a56cd). The committed content may be the previous unchanged copies just being round-tripped, not fresh runs.
evidence.verification_needed: Read the committed summary files to confirm they reflect fresh runs (timestamps inside, not just file mtime).

findings.recommended_action: Operator should decide whether to re-enable sloppytoppy-* (they were disabled in the 5-21 disaster pause per CLAUDE.md note). No code action from tinker.

findings.title: Net velocity 7d = -7 (closed 13, opened 20). Second consecutive negative-net-velocity run, hitting the PROP-030 moderate threshold.

evidence.interpretation: Backlog is 33 — well below the 200 moderate-on-total threshold. Negative net velocity is the artifact of the 2026-05-21 disaster pause (no agent runs for ~3 days while operator unwound the mass-delete). New-issue rate normalized to ~3/day; closure rate is recovering. Expect net velocity to flip positive within 2–3 runs as routed-but-unclosed items (incl. the 3 ISS-21xx items addressed by this report) get closed.

findings.recommended_action: No action — this is monitoring, not a problem yet. If net velocity stays negative for 4+ consecutive runs (PROP-030 major threshold), revisit.

findings.title: Tree-entry count drop: post-recovery commit 1488170d had 4757 entries; HEAD 667e568 has 4374 (delta −383). Cause not fully attributable to dome-prune-integrity (which has no commits matching 'prune' since 5-23).

evidence.depth_limited_investigation: Only --depth 10 cloned; cannot walk back to 1488170d for direct diff. Likely causes (in priority order): (a) closed-issues archive trimming, (b) workspace-sync skip path silently dropping files during the 5-21 to 5-23 abort window, (c) dome-prune-integrity ran but its commits don't match the 'prune' grep, (d) legitimate decider cleanups.
evidence.next_action: Operator with full local clone can run `git diff --name-status 1488170d..HEAD | grep '^D' | head -50` to enumerate. Tinker can do this next run with a deeper clone if disk allows.

findings.recommended_action: Operator-side verification (or next-run tinker with --depth 200). Not blocking but worth a one-pass audit before the next disaster postmortem reference uses '4757 entries' as a baseline.

claude_md_audit.reason: Mode 4 was selected for concrete assigned-tinker work. Worth a careful PROP-051 status reconciliation pass next Mode 3 run.
